title: Miconia roseopetala
taxonomy: Miconia roseopetala belongs to the family Melastomataceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the melastome family. This family includes over 170 genera and more than 5,000 species, many of which are found in tropical and subtropical regions. Miconia roseopetala, specifically, is a genus of flowering plants within the family Melastomataceae. The genus Miconia is composed of approximately 1,000 species, which are native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. Miconia roseopetala's classification as a flowering plant within the Melastomataceae family is a result of its unique characteristics and shared features with other plants in this family.
